disclaimer: This question bank is curated for Deloitte and HashedIn by Deloitte interviews based on candidate experiences from LeetCode discussions and other platforms. Deloitte interviews typically focus on DSA (Easy-Medium), SQL queries, and system design for experienced roles. HashedIn by Deloitte emphasizes LeetCode Medium problems, prefix sums, sliding window, and DP.

questions.title: Two Sum
questions.question: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers that add up to target.
questions.answer: Use a hash map to store complements while iterating O(n) time and O(n) space.
relevant_options: Nested loops for all pairs O(n²)
Sort and use two pointers O(n log n)
Use a hash map to store complements O(n)
Use a stack to track elements

questions.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
questions.question: You are given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on the i-th day. You want to maximize profit by choosing a single day to buy and a different day in the future to sell. Return the maximum profit.
questions.answer: Track minimum price so far and calculate max difference.
relevant_options: Sort array and pick min and max
Track minimum price so far and calculate max difference
Buy at lowest, sell at highest overall

questions.title: Valid Parentheses
questions.question: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid.
questions.answer: Use a stack: push opening brackets, pop and match when closing brackets are encountered.
relevant_options: Count number of each bracket type and compare
Use a stack with a mapping dictionary
Replace valid pairs recursively until empty
Check if string length is even then compare halves

questions.title: Valid Anagram
questions.question: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s.
questions.answer: Count character frequencies in both strings and compare; or sort both strings and compare.
relevant_options: Use hash map to count characters
Use XOR on characters (doesn't work for duplicates)

questions.title: Longest Common Subsequence
questions.question: Given two strings text1 and text2, return the length of their longest common subsequence.
questions.answer: DP table: if match, dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1] + 1; else dp[i][j] = max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).

questions.title: Longest Palindromic Subsequence
questions.question: Given a string s, find the length of the longest palindromic subsequence in s.
questions.answer: DP on intervals: if s[i]==s[j], dp[i][j]=dp[i+1][j-1]+2; else dp[i][j]=max(dp[i+1][j], dp[i][j-1]).
relevant_options: Reverse string and find LCS with original
Expand around center (for substrings, not subsequences)

questions.title: Longest Common Substring
questions.question: Given two strings, find the length of the longest common substring (contiguous).
questions.answer: DP table: if match, dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1]+1; track max. Reset when mismatch.
relevant_options: Sliding window with hash of substrings
Binary search on length with rolling hash

questions.title: Longest Palindromic Substring
questions.question: Given a string s, return the longest palindromic substring.
questions.answer: Expand around center for each possible center (odd and even lengths).

questions.title: Maximum Subarray (Kadane's Algorithm)
questions.question: Given an integer array nums, find the contiguous subarray with the largest sum and return its sum.
questions.answer: Kadane's algorithm: current = max(nums[i], current + nums[i]), track global max.
relevant_options: Prefix sums with brute force O(n²)
Divide and conquer O(n log n)

questions.title: Search in a 2D Matrix
questions.question: Write an efficient algorithm that searches for a value target in an m x n integer matrix. The matrix has the following properties: integers in each row are sorted from left to right, and the first integer of each row is greater than the last integer of the previous row.
questions.answer: Treat as 1D array: binary search using row = mid // n, col = mid % n.
relevant_options: Binary search on rows then on columns
Start from top-right and eliminate row/col
Binary search on each row O(m log n)

questions.title: Search a 2D Matrix II
questions.question: Search for target in matrix where rows and columns are sorted separately.
questions.answer: Start from top-right corner; if current > target move left, if current < target move down.
relevant_options: Binary search on each row O(m log n)
Start from bottom-left instead of top-right

questions.title: Restore IP Addresses
questions.question: Given a string s containing only digits, return all possible valid IP addresses that can be formed by inserting dots into s.
questions.answer: Backtracking: place 3 dots forming 4 segments, validate each segment (0-255, no leading zeros unless segment is '0').

questions.title: Generate all possible valid IP addresses from a given string
questions.question: Same as Restore IP Addresses.
questions.answer: Backtracking: try all placements of dots, validate each octet (0-255, no leading zeros).
relevant_options: Use nested loops for 3 dot positions
Use 4 loops for each segment length

questions.title: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number
questions.question: Given a string containing digits from 2-9 inclusive, return all possible letter combinations that the number could represent.
questions.answer: Backtracking building string, mapping digits to corresponding letter arrays.
relevant_options: BFS queue building combinations level by level

questions.title: Group Anagrams
questions.question: Given an array of strings strs, group the anagrams together.
questions.answer: Sort each string to use as key in hash map; values are lists of original strings.
relevant_options: Character count tuple as key (26-length array)
Sort each word and use map of sorted→list

questions.title: Top K Frequent Elements
questions.question: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the k most frequent elements.
questions.answer: Count frequencies, use min-heap of size k or bucket sort by frequency.
relevant_options: Sort by frequency after counting O(n log n)
Use priority queue with custom comparator
Bucket sort with array of lists

questions.title: Product of Array Except Self
questions.question: Return an array answer such that answer[i] is the product of all elements except nums[i], without division and in O(n).
questions.answer: Compute prefix products and suffix products separately, then multiply for each index.
relevant_options: Divide total product by each element (fails for zeros)
Use two passes: left to right then right to left

questions.title: Container With Most Water
questions.question: Given n non-negative integers representing heights, find two lines that form a container holding the most water.
questions.answer: Two pointers from both ends, move the shorter pointer inward, tracking max area.
relevant_options: Two pointers moving both inward simultaneously
Two pointers moving shorter inward, compute area each step
Use monotonic stack to find boundaries

questions.title: 3Sum
questions.question: Given an integer array nums, return all triplets that sum to zero, without duplicates.
questions.answer: Sort array, fix first element, use two pointers for the remaining to find pairs summing to -first.
relevant_options: Triple nested loops with hash set for deduplication
Sort and use two pointers with skip duplicates
Use hash map for pair sums

questions.title: Merge Intervals
questions.question: Given an array of intervals, merge all overlapping intervals and return the non-overlapping intervals.
questions.answer: Sort by start time, then merge if current start ≤ last end; otherwise add new interval.
relevant_options: Sort by end time and merge backwards
Sort by start and use stack for merging

questions.title: Non-overlapping Intervals
questions.question: Find minimum number of intervals to remove to make the rest non-overlapping.
questions.answer: Sort by end time; greedy: keep intervals that end earliest, remove overlapping.
relevant_options: Sort by start and use heap

questions.title: Meeting Rooms II
questions.question: Find minimum number of meeting rooms required given intervals.
questions.answer: Sort starts and ends separately, two pointers: if start < end, need room else free room.
relevant_options: Sort by start then assign room

questions.title: Number of Islands
questions.question: Given an m x n 2D grid of '1's (land) and '0's (water), count the number of islands.
questions.answer: DFS/BFS on each unvisited '1', marking visited, increment count for each exploration.
relevant_options: Union-Find (Disjoint Set Union) on land cells
BFS with queue for flood fill
DFS recursion (may cause stack overflow for large grids)
Use visited matrix or mark grid in-place

questions.title: Course Schedule
questions.question: Given n courses and prerequisites, determine if all courses can be finished.
questions.answer: Detect cycle in directed graph using DFS with states or Kahn's topological sort.
relevant_options: Union Find (not suitable for directed cycles)

questions.title: Course Schedule II
questions.question: Return the ordering of courses to finish all; return empty array if impossible.
questions.answer: Kahn's BFS topological sort: enqueue nodes with indegree 0, remove edges, add to result.
relevant_options: DFS post-order with stack for ordering
DFS with color states and result list

questions.title: Clone Graph
questions.question: Given a reference of a node in a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy.
questions.answer: BFS or DFS using hash map to map original nodes to cloned nodes, clone neighbors recursively.
relevant_options: Create new nodes and copy adjacency separately

questions.title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
questions.question: Given unsorted array, find longest consecutive elements sequence length in O(n) time.
questions.answer: Use set for O(1) lookup; for each num, if num-1 not in set, it's start of sequence; count consecutive numbers.
relevant_options: Sort and find consecutive runs O(n log n)
Hash set and only count from potential starts

questions.title: Find the Duplicate Number
questions.question: Find duplicate number in array of n+1 integers in range [1,n] with O(1) space.
questions.answer: Floyd's cycle detection (treat array as linked list, value as next index).
relevant_options: Sort and compare O(n log n) modifies array
Binary search on value range O(n log n)
Sum of array minus sum of [1..n] (doesn't work for multiple occurrences)

questions.title: First Missing Positive
questions.question: Find smallest missing positive integer in O(n) time O(1) space.
questions.answer: Place each number in its correct index (nums[i] should be at index nums[i]-1), ignore out of range.
relevant_options: Sort then scan O(n log n)

questions.title: Next Permutation
questions.question: Find next lexicographically greater permutation; if not possible, return sorted order.
questions.answer: Find first decreasing element from right, find next larger element to right, swap, reverse suffix.
relevant_options: Find pattern of descent then ascent

questions.title: Set Matrix Zeroes
questions.question: If an element is 0, set its entire row and column to 0. Do it in-place with O(1) extra space.
questions.answer: Use first row and first column as markers; track if first row/col need zeroing separately.
relevant_options: Use two sets to track rows and cols to zero O(m+n)
Create copy of matrix and check O(mn)
Mark with special sentinel value then convert
Use boolean flags for rows and cols

questions.title: Spiral Matrix
questions.question: Return all elements of an m x n matrix in spiral order.
questions.answer: Track boundaries (top, bottom, left, right), traverse perimeter, shrink each iteration.
relevant_options: BFS with visited set and direction priority

questions.title: Rotate Image
questions.question: Rotate an n x n matrix 90 degrees clockwise in-place.
questions.answer: Transpose the matrix, then reverse each row.
relevant_options: Create new matrix and copy (O(n²) space)

questions.title: Word Search
questions.question: Given a grid and a word, return true if the word exists (adjacent letters, no reuse).
questions.answer: DFS backtracking from each starting cell, marking visited temporarily.
relevant_options: DFS with visited set and pruning

questions.title: Generate Parentheses
questions.question: Given n pairs of parentheses, generate all well-formed combinations.
questions.answer: Backtracking: add '(' if left < n, add ')' if right < left, building result string.
relevant_options: DP building from smaller n combinations

questions.title: Combination Sum
questions.question: Find all unique combinations where numbers sum to target (unlimited use).
questions.answer: Backtracking with index to avoid duplicates, subtract target on each recursive call.
relevant_options: DFS with remain target and start index
Greedy with largest first then backtrack

questions.title: Subsets
questions.question: Return all possible subsets (the power set) of a unique integer array.
questions.answer: Backtracking adding/removing elements, or bitmask iteration from 0 to 2^n-1.

questions.title: Permutations
questions.question: Given an array of distinct integers, return all possible permutations.
questions.answer: Backtracking with used boolean array, add to result when size equals nums length.
relevant_options: Use next_permutation repeatedly on sorted array

questions.title: Decode String
questions.question: Decode encoded string: k[encoded_string] pattern, e.g., '3[a]2[bc]' -> 'aaabcbc'.
questions.answer: Two stacks: one for numbers, one for strings; when ']', pop number and build repeated string.

questions.title: Basic Calculator II
questions.question: Evaluate expression with +, -, *, / and spaces, no parentheses.
questions.answer: Single pass: track last number, operator; for */ compute immediately; for +- push to stack.
relevant_options: Use stack for numbers and sign

questions.title: Simplify Path
questions.question: Given absolute Unix path, simplify it (remove '//', '/.', '/..', trailing slash).
questions.answer: Split by '/', use stack: if '..' pop, if '.' or '' ignore, else push.

questions.title: Reverse Words in a String
questions.question: Reverse the order of words in a string (ignoring leading/trailing spaces).
questions.answer: Split by spaces into words, reverse list, join with single space.
relevant_options: Two pointers on characters to reverse in-place

questions.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
questions.question: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
questions.answer: Sliding window with two pointers and a hash map storing last index of each character.
relevant_options: Generate all substrings and check O(n³)
Sliding window with set and left pointer
Dynamic programming with last occurrence array

questions.title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
questions.question: Find longest substring where same character can be made same by ≤k changes.
questions.answer: Sliding window with frequency map; window size - max frequency ≤ k.
relevant_options: Two pointers with max frequency tracking

questions.title: Minimum Window Substring
questions.question: Find minimum window substring of s containing all characters of t.
questions.answer: Sliding window with two pointers; expand right until all chars found, then shrink left.
relevant_options: Use two pointers with frequency map

questions.title: Sliding Window Maximum
questions.question: Return max of each sliding window of size k.
questions.answer: Monotonic deque (decreasing order) storing indices; pop from front when out of window.
relevant_options: Max-heap with lazy removal O(n log k)

questions.title: Fruit Into Baskets
questions.question: Collect maximum number of fruits with only two types allowed.
questions.answer: Sliding window with two types; expand right, when third type appears shrink left.

questions.title: Partition Labels
questions.question: Partition string into as many parts so each character appears in at most one part.
questions.answer: Store last occurrence of each char, expand segment until current index equals last occurrence.

questions.title: Find All Anagrams in a String
questions.question: Find all start indices of p's anagrams in s.
questions.answer: Sliding window with frequency array; compare counts using matches counter.
relevant_options: Precompute p's frequency and use queue

questions.title: Permutation in String
questions.question: Given two strings s1 and s2, return true if s2 contains a permutation of s1.
questions.answer: Sliding window of length s1.length() with frequency array; compare counts using matches counter.
relevant_options: Sort s1 and all substrings of s2
Use two pointers with frequency map

questions.title: House Robber
questions.question: Maximum amount you can rob from non-adjacent houses.
questions.answer: DP: rob[i] = max(rob[i-1], rob[i-2] + nums[i]), space-optimized to two variables.
relevant_options: Use two variables prev2, prev1, current

questions.title: House Robber II (Circular)
questions.question: Houses in a circle, cannot rob adjacent. Return max amount.
questions.answer: Two cases: rob houses 0 to n-2, and 1 to n-1; take max of both.
relevant_options: DP with state tracking first house picked
Break the circle by considering two linear arrays
DP with three states (first not robbed, could be robbed)

questions.title: Climbing Stairs
questions.question: Count distinct ways to climb n steps with 1 or 2 steps at a time.
questions.answer: Fibonacci-like DP: dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2] with base cases.
relevant_options: Dynamic programming with recursion and memoization

questions.title: Coin Change
questions.question: Return fewest coins needed to make an amount, or -1 if impossible.
questions.answer: DP: dp[i] = min(dp[i-coin] + 1) for each coin, initialize with large value, dp[0]=0.

questions.title: Maximum Product Subarray
questions.question: Find contiguous subarray with largest product (may contain negatives).
questions.answer: Track both max and min product so far (because negative times negative becomes positive).
relevant_options: Kadane's algorithm for sum (doesn't work for product)
Divide and conquer O(n log n)

questions.title: Decode Ways
questions.question: Count number of ways to decode a digit string (A=1, B=2, …, Z=26).
relevant_options: Iterative with two variables space optimization

questions.title: Unique Paths
questions.question: Count unique paths from top-left to bottom-right moving only right or down.
questions.answer: Combinatorics: C(m+n-2, m-1) or DP grid with dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1].

questions.title: Minimum Path Sum
questions.question: Find path from top-left to bottom-right minimizing sum, moving only right or down.
questions.answer: DP: dp[i][j] = grid[i][j] + min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]) with boundary initialization.

questions.title: Jump Game
questions.question: Determine if you can reach the last index given maximum jump lengths.
questions.answer: Greedy: maintain farthest reachable index; if at any point i > farthest, return false.

questions.title: Jump Game II
questions.question: Find minimum number of jumps to reach the last index.
questions.answer: Greedy BFS: track current_end, farthest, jumps++; when i reaches current_end, set current_end = farthest.
relevant_options: Single pass greedy with two pointers

questions.title: Partition Equal Subset Sum
questions.question: Determine if array can be partitioned into two subsets with equal sum.
questions.answer: If total sum odd, return false. Use knapsack DP to check if subset sum = total/2 is achievable.

questions.title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
questions.question: Return the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence.
questions.answer: Patience sorting: maintain tails array, binary search for each num to replace or append.
relevant_options: DP O(n²): LIS ending at each index
Binary search on tails O(n log n)

questions.title: Word Break
questions.question: Determine if string can be segmented into dictionary words.
questions.answer: DP: dp[i] true if s[0:i] segmentable and s[j:i] in dictionary.

questions.title: Edit Distance
questions.question: Find minimum number of operations to convert one string to another.
questions.answer: DP table: if chars match, dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1]; else dp[i][j] = 1 + min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1], dp[i-1][j-1]).

questions.title: Palindrome Partitioning
questions.question: Partition string so every substring is palindrome; return all partitions.
questions.answer: Backtracking: check palindrome for prefix using DP or two pointers, recurse on suffix.

questions.title: Word Ladder
questions.question: Find the length of shortest transformation sequence from beginWord to endWord, changing one letter at a time.
questions.answer: BFS from beginWord, generating all possible next words by changing each position.

questions.title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
questions.question: Find the maximum path sum in a binary tree (path can start and end at any node).
questions.answer: DFS returns max gain from node to leaf; track global max = max(global, node.val + left_gain + right_gain).
relevant_options: Brute force check all paths O(n²)
Post-order traversal with negative branch pruning
Rooted tree DP with two options (include/not include node)

questions.title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree
questions.question: Find the lowest common ancestor of two given nodes in a binary tree.
questions.answer: Recursively search left and right; if both sides return non-null, current is LCA.
relevant_options: Store parent pointers and find intersection
Find paths from root to both nodes and compare

questions.title: Binary Tree Right Side View
questions.question: Return the values of nodes visible when standing on the right side, top to bottom.
questions.answer: BFS level order and take last node of each level, or DFS with level tracking and first right node.
relevant_options: Level order traversal with queue, add rightmost node each level
DFS right-first preorder, add when level not seen
BFS and store entire level then take last

questions.title: Kth Smallest Element in a BST
questions.question: Given the root of a BST and an integer k, return the kth smallest value (1-indexed).
questions.answer: In-order traversal (left-root-right) yields sorted order; return kth element visited.
relevant_options: Augment tree with size of left subtree

questions.title: Validate Binary Search Tree
questions.question: Determine if a binary tree is a valid BST.
questions.answer: In-order traversal and check if values are strictly increasing; or DFS with valid range (min, max).
relevant_options: Recursive with lower and upper bound

questions.title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
questions.question: Design an algorithm to serialize and deserialize a binary tree (any shape).
questions.answer: Use level order BFS with null markers, or preorder with sentinel for null children.
relevant_options: Preorder traversal with delimiters and null markers
Level order with queue storing null

questions.title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
questions.question: Return level order traversal (left to right, level by level).
questions.answer: BFS with queue; process level size, collect nodes level by level.
relevant_options: DFS with level tracking in list of lists
Iterative depth first with stack storing (node, level)

questions.title: Binary Tree Zigzag Level Order Traversal
questions.question: Return zigzag level order: first level left to right, second right to left, etc.
questions.answer: BFS with flag for direction; reverse level list for alternate levels.
relevant_options: Use deque to add from front/back
DFS with list of lists, reverse odd indices

questions.title: Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder Traversal
questions.question: Construct binary tree from preorder and inorder traversal arrays.
questions.answer: First element of preorder is root; find root in inorder; recursively build left and right subtrees.
relevant_options: Use hash map for inorder indices for O(1) lookup

questions.title: Diameter of Binary Tree
questions.question: Return the diameter (longest path between any two nodes) of a binary tree.
questions.answer: Post-order DFS: compute height of each node; diameter = max(diameter, leftHeight + rightHeight).
relevant_options: Compute path for each node O(n²)
Two DFS passes to find farthest nodes

questions.title: Balanced Binary Tree
questions.question: Determine if binary tree is height-balanced (height difference ≤ 1 for every node).
questions.answer: Post-order DFS: return -1 if unbalanced, else height; check diff at each node.
relevant_options: Compute heights of left and right recursively, compare
BFS level order to compute heights
Use global flag and early exit

questions.title: Symmetric Tree
questions.question: Check if binary tree is symmetric around its center.
questions.answer: Recursively compare left.left with right.right, and left.right with right.left.
relevant_options: BFS with queue comparing mirrored nodes

questions.title: Invert Binary Tree
questions.question: Given the root of a binary tree, invert the tree (swap left and right children for every node).
questions.answer: Recursively swap left and right children for each node.
relevant_options: Use queue for BFS level order swap

questions.title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
questions.question: Given the root of a binary tree, return its maximum depth.
questions.answer: Recursively compute 1 + max(leftDepth, rightDepth); or BFS counting levels.

questions.title: Path Sum III
questions.question: Count paths where sum of node values equals target. Path must go downwards.
questions.answer: DFS with hash map storing prefix sums; for current node, answer += map[currentSum - target]; then update map.
relevant_options: BFS with running sum per path
Two loops: root to leaf then count subpaths

questions.title: Flatten Binary Tree to Linked List
questions.question: Flatten binary tree to linked list in-place (right pointers only, left = null) using preorder order.
questions.answer: Reverse preorder (right then left) to avoid overwriting; track prev node and link.
relevant_options: Do preorder then rebuild using pointers
Recursive: flatten left, flatten right, attach to right

questions.title: Populating Next Right Pointers in Each Node
questions.question: Given a perfect binary tree, connect each node to its next right node. Use O(1) extra space.
questions.answer: Use next pointers of current level to traverse next level without queue.
relevant_options: BFS with queue storing nodes level by level
Recursive using left->right and right->next of parent
Iterative using dummy head for each level

questions.title: Number of Connected Components in an Undirected Graph
questions.question: Find the number of connected components given n nodes and edges.
questions.answer: Union Find or DFS/BFS to count number of disjoint sets.
relevant_options: Adjacency list then DFS traversal count

questions.title: Graph Valid Tree
questions.question: Given n nodes and edges, determine if the graph forms a valid tree (connected and acyclic).
questions.answer: Check if edges.length == n-1 and graph is connected (DFS/BFS).
relevant_options: Union Find: if all edges unite nodes without cycle and total edges = n-1
DFS with visited set and parent tracking
BFS to check connectivity and cycle
Check if any node has degree > 2 for tree property?

questions.title: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow
questions.question: Find cells where water can flow to both Pacific (top/left) and Atlantic (bottom/right).
questions.answer: DFS/BFS from Pacific edge and Atlantic edge separately, intersect reachable cells.
relevant_options: DFS from each cell to both oceans (O((mn)²))
Union Find on cells with flow direction
DP with memoization on flow reachability

questions.title: Surrounded Regions
questions.question: Capture all 'O's surrounded by 'X's by flipping them to 'X'.
questions.answer: DFS/BFS from boundary 'O's, mark them as safe; flip remaining 'O's to 'X'.
relevant_options: Union Find to connect boundary O's

questions.title: Rotting Oranges
questions.question: Given grid with 0=empty,1=fresh,2=rotten. Rotten spreads to adjacent each minute. Return minutes until all rot or -1.
questions.answer: Multi-source BFS from all rotten oranges simultaneously, track time.
relevant_options: BFS queue initially with all rottens

questions.title: 01 Matrix
questions.question: Given binary matrix, return matrix where each cell is distance to nearest 0.
questions.answer: Multi-source BFS from all 0s; or DP with two passes (top-left then bottom-right).

questions.title: Network Delay Time
questions.question: Find the minimum time for all nodes to receive a signal from source k.
questions.answer: Dijkstra's algorithm from source, answer = max distance to all nodes.

questions.title: Cheapest Flights Within K Stops
questions.question: Find cheapest price from src to dst with at most K stops.
questions.answer: Bellman-Ford variant (DP on stops) or BFS with priority queue (Dijkstra with stop limit).
relevant_options: Modified Dijkstra with stop count in state
BFS with queue for each stop level
DFS with pruning on stops and cost

questions.title: Reorganize String
questions.question: Rearrange string so no two adjacent characters are same. Return result or empty string.
questions.answer: Max-heap by frequency; pop top two, append both, decrement counts, push back if still >0.

questions.title: Task Scheduler
questions.question: Schedule tasks with cooldown n between same tasks; return total intervals.
questions.answer: Count frequencies; max freq determines idle slots; formula (max-1)*(n+1) + count_max.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons
questions.question: Find minimum arrows to burst all balloons (interval overlap).
questions.answer: Sort by end, shoot arrow at end, burst all intervals containing that point.

questions.title: Merge k Sorted Lists
questions.question: Merge k sorted linked lists into one sorted list.
questions.answer: Min-heap of k heads; pop min, push next from same list; or divide and conquer pair merge.
relevant_options: Compare all heads each time O(kn)
Use priority queue O(n log k)
Divide and conquer merge O(n log k)
Collect all values sort O(n log n)

questions.title: LRU Cache
questions.question: Design an LRU cache with get and put operations in O(1) average time.
questions.answer: Use hash map (key → node) and doubly linked list to maintain order; move accessed nodes to head.
relevant_options: Use LinkedHashMap in Java or OrderedDict in Python
Use queue with timestamp and map
Use two stacks for recent and old
Use array with timestamps and sweep

questions.title: Min Stack
questions.question: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
questions.answer: Use two stacks: main stack and min stack storing current minimum at each level.
relevant_options: Store (value, current min) as pair in single stack
Calculate min difference and store delta
Use min variable and re-scan on pop (O(n))
Use priority queue as secondary structure

questions.title: Implement Trie (Prefix Tree)
questions.question: Implement a trie with insert, search, and startsWith methods.
questions.answer: Trie node contains children array (size 26) and boolean isEnd; insert traverses/creates nodes.
relevant_options: Use hash map for children instead of array
Use linked list of nodes per level
Store all words and do prefix check O(n*m)
Use binary search tree of strings

questions.title: Design Circular Queue
questions.question: Design a circular queue using a fixed-size array.
questions.answer: Use head and tail pointers with modulo arithmetic; maintain count or use one empty slot to distinguish full/empty.
relevant_options: Use two stacks to simulate queue
Use linked list with head and tail
Use array with head/tail and count variable
Use two pointers with modulo and size tracking

questions.title: Find Median from Data Stream
questions.question: Design structure supporting add and find median in O(log n) per operation.
questions.answer: Two heaps: max-heap for lower half, min-heap for upper half; balance sizes.
relevant_options: Insert into list and sort O(n log n)
Use balanced BST (not in standard lib)
Binary search on sorted list O(n) insert

questions.title: Fibonacci Series - Nth term
questions.question: Write a program to generate the nth Fibonacci number or the Fibonacci series up to a given number.
questions.answer: Iterative approach: store previous two numbers, update in loop O(n) time O(1) space.
relevant_options: Recursion with memoization O(n) time O(n) space

questions.title: Valid Number
questions.question: Determine if a string is a valid number (integer, decimal, scientific notation).
questions.answer: Finite state machine or regex: optional sign, digits, optional decimal, optional exponent with sign and digits.

questions.title: String to Integer (atoi)
questions.question: Implement atoi conversion handling whitespace, sign, overflow, non-digit termination.
questions.answer: Trim whitespace, check sign, convert digits, check overflow at each step.

questions.title: Multiply Strings
questions.question: Multiply two numbers given as strings without using built-in big integers.
questions.answer: Simulate multiplication: array of size m+n, nested loops adding digit products.

questions.title: Add Two Numbers (Linked List)
questions.question: Add two numbers represented as reverse-linked lists.
questions.answer: Traverse both lists with carry, create new nodes for sum % 10, carry = sum / 10.
relevant_options: Reverse lists then add then reverse

questions.title: Remove Nth Node From End of List
questions.question: Remove nth node from end of linked list in one pass.
questions.answer: Two pointers with n gap; when fast reaches end, slow is at node before target.
relevant_options: Two passes: find length then remove

questions.title: Reverse Linked List
questions.question: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return its head.
questions.answer: Iterative: maintain prev, current, next pointers; or recursive: reverse rest, attach current.
relevant_options: Store values in array and reassign

questions.title: Linked List Cycle
questions.question: Given head of a linked list, determine if the linked list has a cycle.
questions.answer: Floyd's cycle detection (fast and slow pointers); if they meet, cycle exists.
relevant_options: Two-pointer with fast moving 2 steps, slow 1 step

questions.title: Linked List Cycle II
questions.question: Return the node where cycle begins; if no cycle return null.
questions.answer: Find meeting point using fast/slow, then move one pointer to head, advance both at same speed to find start.

questions.title: Intersection of Two Linked Lists
questions.question: Find node where two singly linked lists intersect.
questions.answer: Two pointers: traverse both, reset to other head when reaching end; they meet at intersection.
relevant_options: Calculate lengths, align tails, traverse together
Use hash set for first list nodes
Reverse both lists and find divergence
Mark visited flag in node (not possible)

questions.title: Palindrome Linked List
questions.question: Check if linked list is palindrome (O(1) space).
questions.answer: Find middle, reverse second half, compare halves.
relevant_options: Copy to array then two pointers
Reverse entire list and compare (O(n) space)

questions.title: Middle of the Linked List
questions.question: Return the middle node of a linked list.
questions.answer: Fast and slow pointers: fast moves 2 steps, slow moves 1 step; when fast reaches end, slow is at middle.
relevant_options: Store nodes in array and take middle index
Two passes: first count, second reach middle

questions.title: Sort List
questions.question: Sort a linked list in O(n log n) time using constant space.
questions.answer: Merge sort on linked list: find middle, sort left and right, merge sorted halves.
relevant_options: Bubble sort on linked list O(n²)
Merge sort with slow/fast pointer for middle

questions.title: Reorder List
questions.question: Reorder list to L0 → Ln → L1 → Ln-1 → L2 → ...
questions.answer: Find middle, reverse second half, merge alternating.
relevant_options: Use array to store nodes then reorder
Two pointers for middle, then reverse

questions.title: Copy List with Random Pointer
questions.question: Deep copy a linked list with random pointers.
questions.answer: Two passes: first create copy nodes with mapping from original to copy; second assign random pointers.
relevant_options: Interweave nodes: copy next to original, then separate

questions.title: Rotate List
questions.question: Rotate a linked list to the right by k places.
questions.answer: Find length, compute k % length, find new tail and new head, break and connect.
relevant_options: Move head step by step O(nk)
Connect tail to head and break at new head

questions.title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted List II
questions.question: Remove all duplicates such that only distinct numbers remain (no duplicates at all).
questions.answer: Use dummy head; skip all nodes that have duplicates; link prev to next distinct node.
relevant_options: Use hash map to count occurrences
Store values in set and rebuild

questions.title: Odd Even Linked List
questions.question: Group odd-indexed nodes followed by even-indexed nodes (in-place, O(1) space).
questions.answer: Two pointers: odd head and even head; link odd to odd.next, even to even.next; join at end.
relevant_options: Collect nodes in two lists then merge

questions.title: Swap Nodes in Pairs
questions.question: Swap every two adjacent nodes and return head.
questions.answer: Recursive: swap first two, attach result of recursion to second's next; or iterative with prev pointer.
relevant_options: Convert to array and swap then rebuild

questions.title: Reverse Nodes in k-Group
questions.question: Reverse nodes in k-group and return head.
questions.answer: Recursively reverse next k nodes; track head of reversed segment and next segment.
relevant_options: Use stack to reverse each group
Convert to array and reverse segments

questions.title: Partition List
questions.question: Partition list so nodes with value < x come before nodes ≥ x, preserving order.
questions.answer: Two dummy heads for less and greater; traverse and append; merge.

questions.title: Add Two Numbers II (Forward order)
questions.question: Add two numbers stored in forward order in linked lists.
questions.answer: Use stacks to push values, then pop and compute with carry, building result from tail.
relevant_options: Compute lengths and pad with zeros

questions.title: Flatten a Multilevel Doubly Linked List
questions.question: Flatten a doubly linked list where nodes may have child pointers.
questions.answer: DFS: recursively flatten child, insert after current node, continue traversal.
relevant_options: Use queue to process nodes in order

questions.title: Convert Binary Number in a Linked List to Integer
questions.question: Given head of linked list with 0/1 bits representing binary number (MSB first), return integer.
questions.answer: Iterate: result = result * 2 + node.val (left shift then add).

questions.title: Design HashSet
questions.question: Design a hash set without using built-in libraries.
questions.answer: Array of buckets with linked lists; compute hash modulo bucket count.
relevant_options: Single boolean array of large size
Use binary search tree per bucket
Use array of ints with bit flags

questions.title: Design HashMap
questions.question: Implement a hash map with get, put, remove operations.
questions.answer: Array of buckets with linked list storing key-value pairs; hash function determines bucket.
relevant_options: Use large array for key range (int)

questions.title: Insert Delete GetRandom O(1)
questions.question: Design data structure supporting insert, delete, getRandom in O(1) average.
questions.answer: HashMap for value→index, ArrayList for values; delete swaps with last element then pops.
relevant_options: Use HashSet for existence, getRandom via list of keys
Use deque and map (delete not O(1))

questions.title: RandomizedSet (without duplicates)
questions.question: Design data structure supporting insert, delete, getRandom in O(1) average (no duplicates).
questions.answer: HashMap for value→index, ArrayList for values; delete swaps with last element then pops.
relevant_options: Use HashSet for existence, getRandom via list of keys
Use deque and map (delete not O(1))

questions.title: Time Based Key-Value Store
questions.question: Store values with timestamps; get returns latest value with timestamp ≤ given.
questions.answer: HashMap from key to list of (timestamp, value) pairs; binary search on timestamps for get.
relevant_options: Use TreeMap for timestamps in sorted order
Use priority queue per key (inefficient for get)
Use sorted dictionary from key to timestamps

questions.title: Snapshot Array
questions.question: Implement array with snapshots; get(index, snap_id) returns value at that snapshot.
questions.answer: Store list of (snap_id, value) per index; use binary search for get; snap returns current snap_id.
relevant_options: Store entire array per snapshot (O(mn) space)
Use tree map for changes per index

questions.title: Flatten Nested List Iterator
questions.question: Implement an iterator to flatten a nested list of integers.
questions.answer: Use stack of iterators; on hasNext, peek; if current is integer return; else recurse on list.
relevant_options: Use queue to collect all integers upfront

questions.title: Peeking Iterator
questions.question: Design an iterator that supports peek() operation in addition to next() and hasNext().
questions.answer: Store next element during peek; return stored value without advancing underlying iterator.
relevant_options: Call next() and store for peek, then return in next
Cache next element in class variable
Override hasNext to cache peek value

questions.title: Moving Average from Data Stream
questions.question: Calculate moving average of last size elements.
questions.answer: Queue with sum variable; add new, remove old if size exceeds, update sum.
relevant_options: Store array and compute each time

questions.title: Implement Stack using Queues
questions.question: Implement stack using only two queues.
questions.answer: Push: O(1) using one queue, rotate elements to bring new to front; or O(n) push with two queues.
relevant_options: Two queues: swap after each push
Single queue: push, rotate size-1 elements
List with pop from end (not using queue)

questions.title: Implement Queue using Stacks
questions.question: Implement a FIFO queue using two stacks.
questions.answer: Two stacks: input for push, output for pop; transfer if output empty.
relevant_options: One stack with reversal each pop (O(n))
Use single stack and recursion for pop

questions.title: Design Circular Deque
questions.question: Design a circular double-ended deque with fixed capacity.
questions.answer: Use array with head, tail, count; modulo arithmetic for wraparound.
relevant_options: Doubly linked list with size limit
Two stacks for front and back
Use Java Deque interface (but not allowed)

questions.title: Basic Calculator
questions.question: Evaluate expression with +, -, (, ), and spaces.
questions.answer: Stack with sign and result; when '(' push current result and sign, reset, then combine on ')'.
relevant_options: Two stacks for numbers and operators
Remove spaces then evaluate from left

questions.title: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
questions.question: Evaluate RPN (postfix) expression.
questions.answer: Use stack: push numbers; when operator, pop two, compute, push result.

questions.title: Daily Temperatures
questions.question: For each day, days until warmer temperature.
questions.answer: Monotonic decreasing stack storing indices.

questions.title: Next Greater Element I
questions.question: Next greater element for subset of array.
questions.answer: Monotonic decreasing stack for nums2, map element to next greater.

questions.title: Next Greater Element II (Circular)
questions.question: Next greater element in circular array.
questions.answer: Double the array conceptually, monotonic stack on indices % n.

questions.title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
questions.question: Given array of bar heights, find area of largest rectangle in histogram.
questions.answer: Monotonic increasing stack storing indices; when smaller height found, pop and compute area.
relevant_options: Brute force check all pairs O(n²)

questions.title: Maximal Rectangle
questions.question: Given binary matrix, find largest rectangle containing only 1's.
questions.answer: Treat each row as base, compute heights, apply largest rectangle in histogram for each row.
relevant_options: For each cell, expand right and down O(n³)
DP with left, right, height arrays
Convert to histogram for each row

questions.title: Trapping Rain Water
questions.question: Given elevation map, compute how much water it can trap.
questions.answer: Two pointers: left max and right max; water += min(leftMax, rightMax) - height[current]; move smaller pointer.
relevant_options: For each bar, water = min(leftMax, rightMax) - height O(n²)
Precompute left and right max arrays O(n) space
Two pointers O(1) space with left/right max tracking

questions.title: Find the Winner of the Circular Game
questions.question: Josephus problem: n friends, eliminate every kth. Return last remaining.
questions.answer: Josephus: f(1)=0, f(n) = (f(n-1)+k)%n. Return +1.

questions.title: Count of Matches in Tournament
questions.question: Number of matches in tournament with n teams, each match eliminates one.
questions.answer: n-1. Or simulate: matches = floor(n/2), n = ceil(n/2).

questions.title: Maximum Population Year
questions.question: Given births and deaths, find year with max population.
questions.answer: Difference array on [birth, death), prefix sum, find max index.
relevant_options: Use map of year to delta

questions.title: Minimum Number of Operations to Convert Time
questions.question: Convert current to correct time using increments of 1,5,15,60 minutes.
questions.answer: Convert both to minutes, diff = correct-current. Greedy: use 60,15,5,1.

questions.title: Count Sorted Vowel Strings
questions.question: Count strings of length n consisting only of vowels in lexicographic order.
questions.answer: Stars and bars: C(n+4,4) or DP: dp[char][len].

questions.title: Ugly Number II
questions.question: Find nth ugly number (divisible by 2,3,5 only).
questions.answer: DP with three pointers: next ugly = min(2*dp[p2], 3*dp[p3], 5*dp[p5]).

questions.title: Happy Number
questions.question: Determine if number is happy (sum squares eventually 1).
questions.answer: Floyd cycle detection: slow and fast pointer; cycle detected if slow == fast and not 1.

questions.title: Count Primes
questions.question: Count number of primes less than n.
questions.answer: Sieve of Eratosthenes: boolean array, mark multiples starting from p².

questions.title: Power of Two
questions.question: Given integer n, return true if it is a power of two.

questions.title: Missing Number
questions.question: Given array of n distinct numbers from 0 to n, find the missing number.
questions.answer: XOR all numbers from 0 to n with array elements, result is missing number.
relevant_options: Sort and check missing O(n log n)

questions.title: Single Number
questions.question: Given non-empty array where every element appears twice except one, find the single one.
questions.answer: XOR all elements together (a XOR a = 0).

questions.title: Single Number II
questions.question: Every element appears three times except one. Find the single one.
questions.answer: Use bitwise: ones, twos variables; ones = (ones ^ num) & ~twos; twos = (twos ^ num) & ~ones.
relevant_options: Sort and scan O(n log n)

questions.title: Single Number III
questions.question: Two numbers appear once, others twice. Find both numbers.
questions.answer: XOR all -> xorResult; find rightmost set bit; partition numbers into two groups based on that bit.

questions.title: Number of 1 Bits
questions.question: Count number of 1 bits (Hamming weight) of unsigned integer.
questions.answer: n & (n-1) clears lowest set bit; count iterations.

questions.title: Reverse Bits
questions.question: Reverse bits of 32-bit unsigned integer.
questions.answer: Mask and swap approach: swap neighboring bits, then 2-bit groups, 4-bit groups, etc.
relevant_options: Loop 32 times shifting result left and adding LSB

questions.title: Counting Bits
questions.question: Return array count of 1 bits for numbers from 0 to n.

questions.title: Sum of Two Integers
questions.question: Calculate sum of two integers without using + or -.
questions.answer: Use XOR for sum, AND for carry; carry <<=1; repeat until carry==0.
relevant_options: Use logarithm and exponent (fails for negative)

questions.title: Hamming Distance
questions.question: Hamming distance between two integers (number of differing bits).
questions.answer: XOR then count bits (popcount).

questions.title: Total Hamming Distance
questions.question: Sum of Hamming distances for all pairs in array.
questions.answer: For each bit position, count ones and zeros; contribution = ones * zeros.
relevant_options: XOR all pairs then sum popcount

questions.title: Maximum XOR of Two Numbers in an Array
questions.question: Find maximum XOR of any two numbers in array.
questions.answer: Use Trie (bitwise) or greedy: for each prefix, check if complement exists.

questions.title: Power of Four
questions.question: Return true if n is power of four.
questions.answer: n>0, (n & (n-1))==0 (power of two) and n & 0x55555555 !=0 (bits in odd positions).

questions.title: Find the Difference
questions.question: String t is string s with one random letter added. Find the added letter.
questions.answer: XOR all characters of s and t, result is added letter.

questions.title: Gray Code
questions.question: Generate Gray code sequence for n bits (consecutive differ by one bit).
questions.answer: Sequence = i xor (i>>1) for i from 0 to 2^n -1.

questions.title: Permutation Sequence
questions.question: Given n and k, return the kth permutation sequence of numbers 1..n.
questions.answer: Compute factorial, determine each digit by (k-1)/factorial, update k and list.

questions.title: Pow(x, n)
questions.question: Implement pow(x, n) with O(log n) time.
questions.answer: Fast exponentiation: binary exponent recursion; handle negative n as 1/x^n.

questions.title: Sqrt(x)
questions.question: Return integer square root of x (floor).
questions.answer: Binary search between 0 and x; check mid*mid ≤ x.

questions.title: Integer to Roman
questions.question: Convert integer to Roman numeral (1 to 3999).
questions.answer: Use mapping of values with special cases (4,9,40,90,400,900).

questions.title: Roman to Integer
questions.question: Convert Roman numeral to integer.
questions.answer: Iterate left to right; if current < next, subtract current; else add current.

questions.title: Factorial Trailing Zeroes
questions.question: Count trailing zeroes in n!.
questions.answer: Count factors of 5: sum floor(n/5) + floor(n/25) + floor(n/125) + ...

questions.title: Maximum Swap
questions.question: Swap two digits once to get maximum number.
questions.answer: Find largest digit from right that can be swapped with smaller digit on left; or use bucket of last occurrence.
relevant_options: Greedy from left: find max digit to right
Convert to array and sort for reference

questions.title: Minimum Number of Swaps to Make the String Balanced
questions.question: String has equal '[' and ']', min swaps to balance.
questions.answer: Track imbalance, answer = (max_imbalance+1)/2.

questions.title: Longest Word in Dictionary through Deleting
questions.question: Find longest word in dictionary that is subsequence of s (lex smallest if tie).
questions.answer: Sort words by length desc then lex; check subsequence using two pointers.

questions.title: Remove K Digits
questions.question: Remove k digits to make smallest possible number (no leading zeros).
questions.answer: Monotonic increasing stack; pop when stack top > current and k>0; remove leading zeros.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Refueling Stops
questions.question: Minimum stops to reach target with given fuel stations.
questions.answer: Max-heap of fuel from passed stations; always go farthest possible, refuel from heap when needed.

questions.title: Car Pooling
questions.question: Given trips [numPassengers, from, to] and capacity, determine if possible.
questions.answer: Difference array: add passengers at from, subtract at to; check cumulative at any point ≤ capacity.

questions.title: Meeting Rooms I
questions.question: Given intervals, determine if a person can attend all meetings (no overlaps).
questions.answer: Sort by start time, check if any interval overlaps with next.
relevant_options: Sort by end time and compare
Use sweep line to detect overlaps

questions.title: Insert Interval
questions.question: Insert new interval into sorted non-overlapping intervals, merge if needed.
questions.answer: Add all intervals before new, merge overlapping, add rest.

questions.title: Summary Ranges
questions.question: Given sorted unique array, return summary ranges.
questions.answer: Two pointers: start of range, extend while consecutive, add range when break.
relevant_options: Create list of pairs then format

questions.title: Missing Ranges
questions.question: Find missing ranges in sorted array from lower to upper inclusive.
questions.answer: Check between lower and first element, then between consecutive, then last and upper.

questions.title: Can Place Flowers
questions.question: Plant n new flowers in flowerbed without violating no-adjacent-flowers rule.
questions.answer: Greedy: iterate, if current is 0 and adjacent are 0 (or boundaries), plant and skip next.

questions.title: Maximum Average Subarray I
questions.question: Find maximum average of subarray of length k.
questions.answer: Sliding window sum, compute average, track max.
relevant_options: Prefix sums then compute each window O(n)

questions.title: Subarray Product Less Than K
questions.question: Count number of contiguous subarrays with product < k.
questions.answer: Sliding window: expand right, adjust left while product ≥ k, count subarrays ending at right.
relevant_options: Binary search on length with product logs

questions.title: Number of Subarrays with Bounded Maximum
questions.question: Count subarrays where max element between L and R inclusive.
questions.answer: Count subarrays with max ≤ R, subtract those with max < L.

questions.title: Longest Turbulent Subarray
questions.question: Find longest subarray where comparison signs alternate.
questions.answer: Track up and down lengths; reset when sign same or equal.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Vowels in a Substring of Given Length
questions.question: Given string and integer k, return maximum vowels in any substring of length k.
questions.answer: Sliding window: count vowels in window, slide, track max.

questions.title: Grumpy Bookstore Owner
questions.question: Given customers and grumpy array, can use X minutes secret technique to suppress grumpiness. Max satisfied customers.
questions.answer: Base satisfaction (grumpy=0 minutes), plus max additional satisfaction from sliding window of size X.
relevant_options: Brute force try all window positions
Prefix sums for base and additional

questions.title: Max Consecutive Ones III
questions.question: Given binary array and k flips (0->1), find longest consecutive 1s after at most k flips.
questions.answer: Sliding window: maintain count of zeros; if zeros > k, shrink window from left.

questions.title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
questions.question: Find minimal length subarray with sum ≥ target (positive integers only).
questions.answer: Sliding window: expand right, shrink left while sum ≥ target, track min length.
relevant_options: Binary search on length with prefix sums
Prefix sums and binary search per start

questions.title: Subarray Sum Equals K
questions.question: Count number of subarrays where sum equals k (may include negatives).
questions.answer: Prefix sum with hash map: map[sum]++ ; ans += map[sum - k] at each step.
relevant_options: Prefix sums array then binary search (not for negatives)

questions.title: Continuous Subarray Sum
questions.question: Check if subarray of length at least 2 has sum divisible by k.
questions.answer: Use prefix sum modulo k: if same remainder seen before and distance ≥ 2, return true.
relevant_options: Prefix sums with set for remainders

questions.title: Find Pivot Index
questions.question: Find pivot index where sum of left equals sum of right (excluding pivot).
questions.answer: Total sum - left sum - nums[i] == left sum; so left sum * 2 == total - nums[i].
relevant_options: Compute left sum and total sum iterate

questions.title: Maximum Size Subarray Sum Equals k
questions.question: Find longest subarray length with sum = k (may include negatives).
questions.answer: Prefix sum with map storing first occurrence of each sum; ans = max(ans, i - map[sum-k]).
relevant_options: Binary search on length with prefix sums

questions.title: Range Sum Query - Immutable
questions.question: SumRange(i,j) returns sum from i to j inclusive.
questions.answer: Precompute prefix sums; sumRange(i,j) = prefix[j+1] - prefix[i].
relevant_options: Store array and sum each query O(n)
DP with 2D table for all ranges

questions.title: Range Sum Query 2D - Immutable
questions.question: Given 2D matrix, implement sumRegion(row1, col1, row2, col2) returning sum of submatrix.
questions.answer: Precompute prefix sums: dp[i][j] = sum from (0,0) to (i-1,j-1); query = dp[r2+1][c2+1] - dp[r1][c2+1] - dp[r2+1][c1] + dp[r1][c1].
relevant_options: Store matrix and compute on each query O(n²)

questions.title: Sort Colors (Dutch National Flag)
questions.question: Sort array with values 0,1,2 in-place with one pass.
questions.answer: Three pointers: low (0), mid (1), high (2); swap 0 to front, 2 to back.

questions.title: Largest Number
questions.question: Arrange numbers to form the largest possible number.
questions.answer: Sort with custom comparator: a+b > b+a (string concatenation).
relevant_options: Convert to string and sort lex reverse

questions.title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
questions.question: Find the kth largest element in unsorted array.
questions.answer: Quickselect (Hoare's selection) with average O(n) or min-heap of size k.
relevant_options: Sort array and pick index O(n log n)
Use max-heap and pop k times

questions.title: Find the Duplicate Number
questions.question: Find duplicate number in array of n+1 integers in range [1,n] with O(1) space.
questions.answer: Floyd's cycle detection (treat array as linked list, value as next index).
relevant_options: Sort and compare O(n log n) modifies array
Binary search on value range O(n log n)
Sum of array minus sum of [1..n] (doesn't work for multiple occurrences)

questions.title: First Bad Version
questions.question: Find first bad version using isBadVersion API.
questions.answer: Binary search: if mid is bad, hi=mid else lo=mid+1.

questions.title: Guess Number Higher or Lower
questions.question: Guess number from 1 to n with guess(int) API (returns -1,0,1).
questions.answer: Binary search; adjust low/high based on return value.

questions.title: Find First and Last Position of Element in Sorted Array
questions.question: Given sorted array, return [first, last] position of target.
questions.answer: Binary search for leftmost (lower bound) and rightmost (upper bound - 1).
relevant_options: Two binary searches with different conditions

questions.title: Find Peak Element
questions.question: Find any peak element (greater than neighbors) in O(log n) time.
questions.answer: Binary search: if mid < mid+1, peak on right; else peak on left (including mid).
relevant_options: Binary search comparing mid with mid+1
Use gradient ascent to find peak

questions.title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
questions.question: Find median of two sorted arrays in O(log(min(m,n))) time.
questions.answer: Binary search on smaller array to find partition point ensuring leftX ≤ rightY and leftY ≤ rightX.
relevant_options: Use two pointers to find kth element

questions.title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array II
questions.question: Search target in rotated sorted array that may contain duplicates. Return boolean.
questions.answer: Modified binary search: if nums[mid] == nums[left] == nums[right], left++/right--; else normal rotation check.

questions.title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array II
questions.question: Given rotated sorted array with duplicates, find minimum element.
questions.answer: Binary search: if mid == right, decrement right (can't decide). Otherwise standard variation.
relevant_options: Use two pointers with equal case

questions.title: H-Index
questions.question: Given citations array, compute h-index (h papers have at least h citations).
questions.answer: Sort descending, find largest h where citations[h-1] >= h.

questions.title: H-Index II
questions.question: Same as H-Index but citations array is sorted ascending.
questions.answer: Binary search: find largest h such that citations[n-h] >= h.

questions.title: Bulls and Cows
questions.question: Return hint for secret and guess: 'xAyB' where x are bulls (correct digit in correct place), y are cows (correct digit in wrong place).
questions.answer: Count bulls, track remaining digits in secret and guess, cows = sum(min(countSecret[i], countGuess[i])) for i=0..9.
relevant_options: Use two passes: bulls first then cows
Use hash map for digit counts
Use array of size 10 for counts

questions.title: Isomorphic Strings
questions.question: Given two strings, determine if they are isomorphic (characters map one-to-one).
questions.answer: Use two hash maps to track s→t and t→s mappings; or use array of 256 with last seen index.

questions.title: Ransom Note
questions.question: Given ransomNote and magazine, return if ransomNote can be constructed from magazine letters.
questions.answer: Count magazine letters; decrement for ransomNote; all counts non-negative.

questions.title: First Unique Character in a String
questions.question: Given string s, find the first non-repeating character and return its index.
questions.answer: Count frequencies in first pass; second pass finds first with count 1.

questions.title: Find the Index of the First Occurrence in a String
questions.question: Given two strings needle and haystack, return index of first occurrence of needle in haystack.
questions.answer: Slide window of length needle over haystack and compare.

questions.title: Length of Last Word
questions.question: Given string s consisting of words and spaces, return the length of the last word.
questions.answer: Trim trailing spaces, then count from end until space.
relevant_options: Split by spaces and take last

questions.title: Excel Sheet Column Number
questions.question: Convert Excel column title to number (1-indexed).
questions.answer: Iterate: result = result*26 + (c - 'A' + 1).

questions.title: Excel Sheet Column Title
questions.question: Convert integer to Excel column title (A, B, …, Z, AA, AB).
questions.answer: While n>0: n--, char = 'A' + n%26, n /= 26.

questions.title: Zigzag Conversion
questions.question: Convert string to zigzag pattern with given rows then read row-wise.
questions.answer: Put characters into rows using direction change at first and last row.

questions.title: String Compression
questions.question: Compress char array by replacing consecutive duplicates with char and count (if >1).
questions.answer: Two pointers: read pointer, write pointer; count consecutive, write char and if count>1 write digits.
relevant_options: Create new string then copy back

questions.title: Check if a String Contains All Binary Codes of Size K
questions.question: Given binary string s and integer k, return true if all binary codes of length k appear as substrings.
questions.answer: Use rolling hash to generate all substrings of length k, store in set. Check set size == 2^k.
relevant_options: Use sliding window with boolean array

questions.title: Compare Version Numbers
questions.question: Compare two version numbers (e.g., 1.01 and 1.1).
questions.answer: Split by '.' and compare integer values of each revision, filling missing with 0.
relevant_options: Split by dot and compare length

questions.title: Minimum Add to Make Parentheses Valid
questions.question: Find minimum parentheses to add to make string valid.
questions.answer: Track balance; when balance < 0, add one and reset balance to 0; total adds = balance + count of resets.
relevant_options: Greedy count needed opens and closes

questions.title: Score of Parentheses
questions.question: Compute score: '()'=1, AB = A+B, (A)=2*A.
questions.answer: Use stack: '()' pushes 1; on ')', pop until '(', compute sum, multiply by 2 if not first.
relevant_options: Track depth and multiply powers of 2
Use balance variable and add when '()' appears

questions.title: Minimum Remove to Make Valid Parentheses
questions.question: Remove minimum parentheses to make string valid, return result.
questions.answer: Use stack to track invalid indices, then build result string skipping those indices.
relevant_options: Two passes: left to right and right to left

questions.title: Reverse String
questions.question: Write a function that reverses a string in-place (character array).
questions.answer: Use two pointers: swap characters at left and right, move inward.
relevant_options: Use two pointers to swap in-place

questions.title: Reverse Vowels of a String
questions.question: Reverse only the vowels in a string.
questions.answer: Two pointers from both ends, find vowels and swap.
relevant_options: Store vowels in list, reverse, replace

questions.title: Defanging an IP Address
questions.question: Given a valid IPv4 address, return a defanged version where every '.' is replaced with '[.]'.
questions.answer: Use replace function or iterate and replace.

questions.title: Jewels and Stones
questions.question: You're given strings jewels and stones. Count how many stones are also jewels.
questions.answer: Put jewels in a set, count stones that are in set.

questions.title: Shuffle String
questions.question: Given string s and array indices that specify new position of each character, return shuffled string.
questions.answer: Create result char array; place s.charAt(i) at result[indices[i]].
relevant_options: Sort pairs of chars and indices
Use map of index to char

questions.title: Check If Two String Arrays are Equivalent
questions.question: Given two string arrays, return true if the concatenated strings are equal.
questions.answer: Concatenate using StringBuilder or join and compare.

questions.title: Goal Parser Interpretation
questions.question: Interpret string where 'G' → 'G', '()' → 'o', '(al)' → 'al'.
questions.answer: Replace '()' with 'o' and '(al)' with 'al'.

questions.title: Count Items Matching a Rule
questions.question: Count items in 2D array matching rule (type/color/name).
questions.answer: Map ruleKey to index: type=0, color=1, name=2. Count items where item[index] == ruleValue.

questions.title: Subtract the Product and Sum of Digits of an Integer
questions.question: Given integer n, return product of digits minus sum of digits.
questions.answer: Extract digits using %10, compute product and sum, return diff.

questions.title: Kids With the Greatest Number of Candies
questions.question: Given candies array and extraCandies, return boolean array indicating if giving extra to that kid makes them have max candies.
questions.answer: Find current max. For each, check if candies[i] + extraCandies >= max.

questions.title: Number of Steps to Reduce a Number to Zero
questions.question: Given integer num, steps to zero: if even divide by 2, else subtract 1.
questions.answer: Count steps in loop. Optimize: bitCount plus highestBitPosition -1.

questions.title: Decompress Run-Length Encoded List
questions.question: Given list where nums[2*i] = freq, nums[2*i+1] = val. Decompress into list of val repeated freq times.
questions.answer: Iterate i=0 to n step 2, add nums[i+1] repeated nums[i] times.

questions.title: Matrix Diagonal Sum
questions.question: Return sum of primary and secondary diagonal in square matrix, without double-counting center.
questions.answer: Sum primary: arr[i][i]. Sum secondary: arr[i][n-1-i]. If n odd, subtract center once.

questions.title: Find Words That Can Be Formed by Characters
questions.question: Given words and chars string, count lengths of words that can be formed using chars.
questions.answer: Count chars frequency. For each word, check if sufficient for each character.

questions.title: Check if One String Swap Can Make Strings Equal
questions.question: Return true if two strings can be made equal with at most one swap.
questions.answer: Find indices where diff. Must be 0 or 2 diffs, and swapped chars equal.

questions.title: Count the Number of Consistent Strings
questions.question: Given allowed string and array of words, count words consisting only of allowed characters.
questions.answer: Put allowed chars in set. For each word, check if all chars in set.

questions.title: Truncate Sentence
questions.question: Truncate sentence s to first k words.
questions.answer: Split by space, take first k, join with space.

questions.title: Check if Number Has Equal Digit Count and Digit Value
questions.question: For digit i, count of i in string should equal digit at position i.
questions.answer: Count frequencies, compare freq[i] == num[i] for all i.
relevant_options: Use map of char to int

questions.title: Find the Winner of the Circular Game
questions.question: Josephus problem: n friends, eliminate every kth. Return last remaining.
questions.answer: Josephus: f(1)=0, f(n) = (f(n-1)+k)%n. Return +1.

questions.title: Shuffle the Array
questions.question: Given array in form [x1,x2,...,xn,y1,y2,...,yn], return [x1,y1,x2,y2,...].
questions.answer: Use two pointers: one at start, one at mid, build new array.

questions.title: Number of Good Pairs
questions.question: Count good pairs (i<j) where nums[i] == nums[j].
questions.answer: Use frequency map; for each count c, add c*(c-1)/2.
relevant_options: Use array of size 101 if bounded

questions.title: How Many Numbers Are Smaller Than the Current Number
questions.question: For each number, count how many numbers are smaller than it.
questions.answer: Sort copy, use map to store first occurrence index, then map original values.

questions.title: Create Target Array in the Given Order
questions.question: Given indices and values arrays, insert values at indices into result.
questions.answer: Use list insert operation (O(n²) worst case) or use arraylist/vector.
relevant_options: Use linked list for efficient insertion
Use Fenwick tree with binary search

questions.title: Check if Array Is Sorted and Rotated
questions.question: Check if array was sorted in non-decreasing order and then rotated.
questions.answer: Count number of times nums[i] > nums[i+1]; should be ≤1 (with wrap-around check).

questions.title: Minimum Number of Operations to Move All Balls to Each Box
questions.question: Compute moves needed to move all balls to each box.
questions.answer: Two passes: left to right cumulate moves, right to left cumulate moves.
relevant_options: Prefix sums of counts and distances

questions.title: Maximum Element After Decreasing and Rearranging
questions.question: Rearrange array so arr[0]=1, adjacent diff ≤1, each ≤ original. Maximize last element.
questions.answer: Sort, set arr[0]=1, for i: arr[i] = min(arr[i], arr[i-1]+1).

questions.title: Minimum Time to Make Rope Colorful
questions.question: Remove balloons to avoid consecutive same colors with costs. Min total cost.
questions.answer: Group consecutives, keep max cost in group, sum rest.

questions.title: Maximum Product of Two Elements in an Array
questions.question: Find max (nums[i]-1)*(nums[j]-1) for i≠j.
questions.answer: Find top two largest values; multiply (max1-1)*(max2-1).
relevant_options: Use heap to get two largest

questions.title: Count Number of Pairs With Absolute Difference K
questions.question: Count pairs |nums[i]-nums[j]| == k.
questions.answer: Use frequency map; for each num, count num+k and num-k.

questions.title: Find First Palindromic String in the Array
questions.question: Return first palindromic string in array, or empty string.
questions.answer: For each word, check palindrome (two pointers). Return first.

questions.title: Check if All Characters Have Equal Number of Occurrences
questions.question: Return true if all characters in string appear same number of times.
questions.answer: Count frequencies with map, collect values to set, check size==1.

questions.title: Redistribute Characters to Make All Strings Equal
questions.question: Check if you can redistribute characters across n strings to make all equal.
questions.answer: Total count of each char must be divisible by n.

questions.title: Sorting the Sentence
questions.question: Restore sentence from words with numbers at end indicating original position.
questions.answer: Split, create array of size max number, place word without last char at index-1, join.
relevant_options: Use map of number to word

questions.title: Decode the Message
questions.question: Decode message using key mapping: first occurrence of each letter in key maps to a-z order.
questions.answer: Build mapping dict by iterating key, assign next unused letter. Translate message.

questions.title: Check if All Integers in a Range Are Covered
questions.question: Check if all numbers in range are covered by given intervals.
questions.answer: Difference array or boolean array marking, then check all covered.

questions.title: Find the K-Beauty of a Number
questions.question: Count substrings of length k that divide the original number.
questions.answer: Convert to string, slide window of size k, convert to int, check divisibility (non-zero).

questions.title: Maximum Number of Words You Can Type
questions.question: Given broken letters, count words that can be typed fully.
questions.answer: Put broken letters in set. For each word, check if any char in set.

questions.title: Find if Path Exists in Graph
questions.question: Given n vertices and edges, check if path exists from source to destination.
questions.answer: Build adjacency list, BFS/DFS from source to destination.

questions.title: Find Center of Star Graph
questions.question: Find center of star graph (center appears in every edge).
questions.answer: Center appears in every edge. Return common node in first two edges.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Balls in a Box
questions.question: Box number = sum of digits. Find box with max balls for numbers 1 to n.
questions.answer: Compute digit sum for each ball, use counting array (max sum 9*5=45).

questions.title: Reformat Date
questions.question: Convert '20th Oct 2052' to '2052-10-20'.
questions.answer: Parse day (remove st/nd/rd/th), month mapping, year.

questions.title: Design an Ordered Stream
questions.question: Insert (id, value), return list of consecutive values starting from pointer.
questions.answer: Store in array, advance pointer while next exists.

questions.title: Count Pairs of Similar Strings
questions.question: Two strings are similar if they consist of same set of characters. Count pairs.
questions.answer: Convert each string to bitmask (26 bits). Use map of mask to count. Sum count*(count-1)/2.
relevant_options: Use set of chars and compare

questions.title: Largest Odd Number in String
questions.question: Return largest odd number that is substring of given numeric string.
questions.answer: Find last odd digit, return substring from start to that index.
relevant_options: Scan from end for odd digit

questions.title: Minimum Sum of Two Numbers Formed From Digits
questions.question: Distribute digits of array into two numbers to minimize sum.
questions.answer: Sort digits, assign alternately to two numbers.
relevant_options: Put smallest digits in highest place

questions.title: Replace All Digits with Characters
questions.question: String alternates letter-digit. Replace digit with shift of previous letter.
questions.answer: Iterate, if digit, newChar = prevChar + digitValue.

questions.title: Maximum Value of a String in an Array
questions.question: Value = length if contains letters, else integer value. Return max.
questions.answer: If string contains non-digit, value = length; else parse int. Return max.

questions.title: Count Prefixes of a Given String
questions.question: Count words that are prefixes of given string s.
questions.answer: For each word, if s.startsWith(word), count++.

questions.title: Remove Trailing Zeros From a String
questions.question: Remove trailing zeros from numeric string.
questions.answer: Find last index where char != '0', return substring.

questions.title: Determine if String Halves Are Alike
questions.question: Check if both halves have same number of vowels.
questions.answer: Count vowels in first half and second half, compare.

questions.title: Intersection of Multiple Arrays
questions.question: Given 2D array, return intersection of all subarrays.
questions.answer: Start with set of first array, intersect with each subsequent using retainAll.

questions.title: Remove Palindromic Subsequences
questions.question: String only a and b. Remove palindromic subsequence. Min steps to empty.
questions.answer: If empty -> 0, if palindrome -> 1, else 2.

questions.title: Longest Nice Substring
questions.question: Nice substring has both uppercase and lowercase of each letter. Return longest.
questions.answer: Divide and conquer: find chars missing pair, split, recurse.

questions.title: Minimum Difference Between Highest and Lowest of K Scores
questions.question: Choose k scores, minimize difference between max and min.
questions.answer: Sort, slide window of size k, compute diff = nums[i+k-1]-nums[i], track min.

questions.title: Find All K-Distant Indices in an Array
questions.question: For each occurrence of key, add indices within k distance.
questions.answer: Find all indices of key, for each, add range [i-k, i+k] to set.
